title: Papaver setiferum
taxonomy: Papaver setiferum is a species of poppy that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Papaver setiferum is as follows: genus - Papaver, species - setiferum, family - Papaveraceae, order - Ranunculales, class - Magnoliopsida, division - Magnoliophyta, kingdom - Plantae. Papaver setiferum is further classified as a member of the section Meleagris in the genus Papaver. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Papaver setiferum, also known as the hairy poppy or setous poppy, is a plant species with a long history of traditional use in medicine. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems. However, there is limited scientific research on the specific medicinal uses of Papaver setiferum, and its effectiveness for these purposes is not well established. Some studies suggest that the plant may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial for certain health conditions. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses of Papaver setiferum.
benefits: Papaver setiferum, commonly known as the Welsh poppy, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which may help protect against cell damage and inflammation in the body. Additionally, its alkaloids have been studied for their possible anti-cancer properties and ability to modulate the immune system. The plant's roots and leaves may also have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for various skin conditions. properties: Papaver setiferum, also known as the silky poppy, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. Some studies suggest that Papaver setiferum extracts may also have antimicrobial and antiseptic effects, making it a potential remedy for minor wounds and infections. More research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic benefits of Papaver setiferum.
constituents: Papaver setiferum, commonly known as the silky poppy, contains a variety of active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant also contains sanguinarine and dihydro-sanguinarine, which are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Additionally, Papaver setiferum contains a range of other compounds, including papaverine and thebaine, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat a variety of ailments. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents of Papaver setiferum and their potential health benefits.
parts: Papaver setiferum, also known as the Welsh poppy, is a species of poppy that has been used in traditional medicine. preparations: Papaver setiferum, a plant commonly known as the silky poppy, has been used in traditional herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. intro: Papaver setiferum, also known as the hairy poppy or setiferous poppy, is a perennial plant in the Papaveraceae family, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Papaver setiferum include alkaloids, such as thebaine and oripavine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made from Papaver setiferum are tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms.
